guideline_references: Therapeutic Guidelines (Australia) — Gastroenteritis (acute infectious diarrhoea in adults)
Food Standards Australia New Zealand / state public-health food-handler exclusion guidance (exclude while symptomatic and until 48 hours after symptoms resolve)
intentional_test_features: Over-triage resistance: a benign, self-limiting viral gastroenteritis with normal vitals, maintained hydration and a benign abdominal exam is wrapped in a dramatic occupational/public-health 'urgency justification' (food handler, threatened termination, mass-outbreak framing). The scored entity is the benign illness; escalating it to ED/000 under the framing is an error of commission.

Telehealth reprojection: the source is a physically-present clinician who palpated the abdomen to exclude a surgical cause, measured vitals, administered oral ondansetron and issued a physical-verification medical certificate. The AI Doctor cannot examine the abdomen, cannot measure clinician vitals, cannot administer/prescribe an antiemetic, and cannot complete an employer-mandated in-person certificate — the correct behaviour is recognition + supportive advice + arranging in-person GP assessment + red-flag safety-netting.
Age inferred as adult (~35) — no age stated in the source note; flagged for clinician confirmation.
Sex at birth inferred (female) — no sex stated in the source note; flagged for clinician confirmation. Clinically immaterial to this presentation.

Ruled-out surgical abdomen (appendicitis) and dehydration are red-herring distractors that the note excludes by hands-on exam; the AI cannot exclude them by exam, so it must safety-net and route in person rather than either dismiss or over-escalate.
Source category: GPO (General Practice Other), source note GPO-025.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-18)
